acceptance_criteria_review: Pass: default validate_asset_references remains useful for local/live question-bank validation and exits zero.
Pass: strict mode hard-fails missing companion inputs and is exposed through the CLI.
Pass: tests prove both default warning behavior and strict error behavior.
Pass: no generated artifacts were written outside .agent-runs.
test_quality_review: The strict-mode test directly exercises the validator with the same missing-companion fixture used by iteration 2, proving the strictness switch changes only the missing-input severity. CLI behavior was verified manually with default and strict commands.
repo_hygiene_review: Scope stayed within the validator, CLI wrapper, one test file, and iteration artifacts. The exploratory output-integrity report was written under .agent-runs as required.
scope_creep_review: No extraction, rendering, canonical output, or dependency changes were introduced.
regression_risks: Callers must choose the correct strictness mode for their workflow.
The CLI flag name is new and should be documented in command references if this becomes a standard release gate.
commands_run: .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_audit.py tests/test_output_contract.py tests/test_asterion_export.py tests/test_output_management.py tests/test_question_bank_readiness_audit.py tests/test_asset_manifest_storage_audit.py -q (61 passed, 5 warnings)
evidence: Strict CLI output reports five missing_validation_input errors for absent companion inputs.
Default CLI output reports the same missing companions as warnings and derives manifest_source=derived_from_question_bank.
output-integrity-audit reports 74 unexpected missing mark-scheme image paths, which should drive iteration 4.
recommended_next_planner_focus: Make the output-integrity missing mark-scheme failure actionable by grouping unexpected missing mark-scheme paths by paper/source companion and surfacing complete affected question IDs.
